evals.prompt: Can we turn on the Healthcare Operators campaign tomorrow? Give me a go or no-go and exactly what blocks it.
evals.input: Synthetic proposed campaign package: identity=Healthcare Operators v2; 4 assigned senders each have stored 30-day bounce below 1.5%; all 20 sampled templates render; tracking domain is verified; schedule is 09:00-15:00 America/New_York; daily cap is 30 per sender; exact unsent lead supply is 360; required first_name and company_name are complete. Threshold provenance: Launch Runbook v3 approved by the account owner says block when any sender bounce exceeds 5%, when template rendering is below 100%, or when exact runway is under 7 send days.
evals.expected_output: A campaign-specific ready, ready_with_warnings, or blocked verdict with blockers first, warnings, a passed_checks section, threshold provenance, required fixes, and measurement setup.
assertions: The verdict is ready, ready_with_warnings, or blocked
Sender inventory, templates, tracking, guardrails, schedule, and lead supply are checked
Unknown lead supply is not presented as exact runway
The output names threshold provenance and includes passed_checks

objective_checks.description: Returns the blocked verdict required by the three-day runway against a seven-day gate.
Rejects a claim that the campaign is not blocked.
Includes an explicit passed_checks section for satisfied gates.
Cites Launch Runbook v3 as the source of the launch thresholds.
Rejects claims that Launch Runbook v3 is missing, unavailable, or unapproved as the threshold source.
Calculates the three-day runway and compares it with the seven-day gate.
Names lead supply or runway as the launch blocker.
Provides a concrete fix for the insufficient lead-supply runway.
Defines primary metric, guardrails, and a read window for launch measurement.

evals.prompt: This campaign is live. Define the exact monitoring, stop, iterate, and scale rules for the experiment.
evals.input: Synthetic live experiment: 80 delivered, 2 positive replies, 1.1% bounce, no rendering failures, and audience role is the only changed variable. Threshold provenance: the approved Experiment Plan EP-17 defines stop at 0 positive replies after 200 delivered, pause immediately above 5% sender bounce, iterate at 1-3% positive reply rate after 250 delivered, and scale at or above 4% positive reply rate after 250 delivered while bounce stays below 2%. Review daily and make the first decision at 250 delivered.
evals.expected_output: An operational measurement contract that cites EP-17 threshold provenance, separates the primary metric from guardrails, lists passed_checks, preserves the read window, and states exact stop, iterate, and scale rules.
assertions: The primary metric and guardrails are distinct
The minimum read window and review point are explicit
Scale is not based on aggregate rank alone
The output names threshold provenance and includes passed_checks

objective_checks.description: Cites EP-17 as threshold provenance rather than presenting arbitrary thresholds.
Includes an explicit passed_checks section for current operational checks.

objective_checks.pattern: (?:0|zero) positive repl(?:y|ies).{0,100}200 delivered|200 delivered.{0,100}(?:0|zero) positive repl(?:y|ies)

objective_checks.description: States the four-percent-after-250 scale rule with the bounce guardrail.
objective_checks.pattern: (?:4%|four percent).{0,140}250 delivered[\s\S]{0,240}(?:2%|two percent).{0,80}bounce|250 delivered.{0,140}(?:4%|four percent)[\s\S]{0,240}(?:2%|two percent).{0,80}bounce

evals.prompt: Close out this campaign test and give me the learning handoff plus a client-safe update.
evals.input: Synthetic closed experiment EP-17: hypothesis=Finance Operations targeting improves positive reply quality; changed variable=target role; fixed variables=offer, CTA, sequence, sender pool, and schedule; final result=14 positive replies from 280 delivered (5.0%), bounce=1.2%, zero rendering failures. Threshold provenance: approved EP-17 scale gate was at least 4% positive replies after 250 delivered with bounce below 2%. Passed integrity checks are fixed variables preserved, minimum read window reached, and no operational invalidation.
evals.expected_output: A learning record and separate client-safe update that preserve the hypothesis and fixed variables, cite EP-17 threshold provenance, include passed_checks, compare results with the approved gate, and name the next owner and action.
assertions: The original hypothesis and experiment integrity are preserved
Internal operational detail is separated from client-safe language
New evidence is routed back to the appropriate SendLens skill
The output names threshold provenance and includes passed_checks

objective_checks.description: Records the exact synthetic positive-reply and bounce results.
Cites EP-17 as the source of the scale decision threshold.
Includes an explicit passed_checks section for experiment integrity.
Provides a separately labeled client-safe update.
